title: Evangelion Manga Volume 11 Post Script
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion Manga Volume 11: Post Script             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Mon Dec 01, 2008 12:44 pm Well, I think I just made the 12 hour mark. This is the Post Script for the newest volume of the Evangelion manga, the Post Script is written by Carl Horn. EvaGeeks and EvaMonkey are mentioned in it on page 5 of the Post Script. http://i262.photobucket.com/albums/ii112/IrkenEvangelion/PostScript1.jpg http://i262.photobucket.com/albums/ii112/IrkenEvangelion/PostScript2.jpg http://i262.photobucket.com/albums/ii112/IrkenEvangelion/PostScript3.jpg http://i262.photobucket.com/albums/ii112/IrkenEvangelion/PostScript4.jpg http://i262.photobucket.com/albums/ii112/IrkenEvangelion/PostScript5.jpg http://i262.photobucket.com/albums/ii112/IrkenEvangelion/PostScript6.jpg http://i262.photobucket.com/albums/ii112/IrkenEvangelion/PostScript7.jpg

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Dec 01, 2008 1:37 pm This was an interesting read but Carl got one thing wrong: Sadamoto himself stated that the anime was first. "Going Evageeks on you"? hehehe. Nice!

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Dec 01, 2008 3:42 pm The anime started production first. Carl could have meant that the manga came out first.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Mon Dec 01, 2008 3:44 pm From what I heard the only reason the manga started at all was to drum up some advertising for the series.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Dec 01, 2008 3:53 pm master_lloyd wrote: From what I heard the only reason the manga started at all was to drum up some advertising for the series.

[]:             Quote Post by Dartz » Mon Dec 01, 2008 3:58 pm We'll never hear the end of it...

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Tue Dec 02, 2008 12:20 am Hey, I was just noticing: when was the last time we had twelve guests on at this time of night? Not any time in the last few months, that's all I know.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Tue Dec 02, 2008 4:48 pm judging from how Carl gives user numbers as of August 2008, I seriously think he just slipped in this reference at the last minute in Octoberish of last year. my how things change

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Thu Dec 04, 2008 7:35 am Who is Carl Horn by the way? (Can't see the image, due to the fact that I am in school- damn the censorship)

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Dec 04, 2008 9:04 am Lol. He mentioned the Panties Thread.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Thu Dec 04, 2008 9:13 am The Imperialist wrote: Who is Carl Horn by the way? (Can't see the image, due to the fact that I am in school- damn the censorship)

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Thu Dec 04, 2008 10:27 am The Imperialist wrote: Who is Carl Horn by the way?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Dec 04, 2008 1:51 pm When it was actually board cast?! Holy crap! Carl is really hardcore! Anyway, back on topic please .

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Fri Dec 05, 2008 3:44 pm Eitherway, I think Sadamoto would reveal quite a lot whenever he manages to finish the manga (whether in the actual thing, or as part of the Atogaki)

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sat Dec 06, 2008 1:00 am yes, I am shocked and amazed that Carl Horn actually was one of the "1st Gen" fans who watched the series as it came over one gloriously bad fansub at a time bootlegged from Japan week by week; the "Good Ol' Days" of yore when anime was so hard to obtain that anything was a treasure, much less, Eva. (all the 1st Gen fansites are dead; EvaMonkey, EvaCommentary, EvaGeeks, and AnimeNation forums, are all "2nd Gen" fansites. The difference is that 1st Gen revolved around the actual release, while 2nd Gen revolved around various DVD releases)

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Dec 06, 2008 3:30 am eva.onegeek.org isn't 1st gen? What about evaotaku? They didn't have many webhosts back then so not just anybody can pay $30 a month and have a website hosted with a content manager and high bandwidth. Most fan sites were on hosts such as geocities or tripod.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sat Dec 06, 2008 10:24 am how many still get updated? the fact that several Gen1 geocities pages still exist from 2001 (the Stone Age) simply because no one has taken the content down, but they haven't been updated in all that time, doesn't mean its "there" anymore

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Sat Dec 06, 2008 10:40 am How did English speakers get hold of eva back in '96 then? Were they getting fan-subbed VHS or spending a few days downloading an episode on pre-broadband internet (was that even possible back then?!). Please tell me I'm genuinely interested!

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sat Dec 06, 2008 10:51 am dude....they used fansubbed VHS tapes. Granted I guess some must have used the internet, but it really wasn't up to specifications back then; it was more for coordinating where to pick up your VHS tape.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Sat Dec 06, 2008 11:05 am And to think we complain about the time it's taking to release Rebuild on DVD. We don't know we're born.
